commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the omitted live-session scope fields and regression; the compatibility/security tradeoff still needs maintainer review before merge.; Needs attention: The patch hardens mutable-header spoofing, but incomplete live-session scope fingerprinting can preserve stale token-bound authorization context.; Review finding: [P1] Include every scoped token field in live-session reuse — s... commands.review_summary:
The PR moves MCP loopback scope from generated x-* request headers and OPENCLAW_MCP_* env values into server-issued scoped bearer tokens, updates CLI live-session handling and live probes, and adds gateway/CLI regressions.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +130. Total +237 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The central spoofing bug is source-reproducible on current main through header-derived MCP loopback scope, and the new stale live-session bug is source-reproducible because the reuse fingerprint omits five fields carried by the scoped token.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the omitted live-session scope fields and regression; the compatibility/security tradeoff still needs maintainer review before merge.
